ordering_strategy: Wave 1 = primitives/sections with no internal deps (all run in parallel). Wave 2 = page routes that import Wave-1 outputs (all run in parallel). No Wave 3 needed — scout confirms PopularCoursesSubGrid/GuidedPathSection/AccreditationsSection/WhyBodhiSection/PopularCoursesSection/TestimonialsSection all reuse existing files unchanged, and ProgramsIntroSection collapses into PopularCoursesSection with a different eyebrow.

tasks.title: Extend ProgramCard with variant='article' and modeBadge prop

spec.purpose: Add two new presentation toggles to the existing ProgramCard so it covers (a) the floating mode pill seen on listing pages and (b) the lean editorial 'article' card used on the Tips page — without duplicating the component.

props_shape.href?: string (already supported on CTA; in 'article' variant, the whole card becomes the link)

behavior.course_variant: Unchanged from today — image + title + meta row (icon + dot separators) + dashed-divider footer + optional instructor + 'View Program ->' CTA.
behavior.article_variant: Image + title only. No meta row. No instructor row. No 'View Program' CTA. Title swaps from CardTitle h5 to a dark-green heading (use text-text-brand-deep). Whole card is wrapped in a Link to href.
behavior.modeBadge: Floating pill anchored top-right of the card image (top-3 right-3, absolute). bg-surface-1 backdrop-blur, rounded-full, px-3 py-1, text-mini uppercase tracking-wide, text-text-primary. Renders independently of variant.

non_goals: Do NOT change existing default behavior or props — every current call site must still compile.
Do NOT introduce a new ui/article-card.tsx file.
Do NOT change CardTitle, CardFooter, or other shared chrome — only branch via variant inside ProgramCard.

acceptance: Existing call sites in apps/web/src/components/sections/popular-courses-section.tsx and apps/web/src/app/page.tsx compile and render identically (no visual diff in default variant).
When variant='article' is passed, no meta-chip row, no dashed divider, no 'View Program' CTA is rendered.
When variant='article', the title uses text-text-brand-deep and is wrapped in the same Link as href.
When modeBadge={{ label: 'Online' }} is passed, a floating pill renders top-right on the image with bg-surface-1, text-text-primary, text-mini, uppercase. No inline hex.
TypeScript compiles with no new errors (yarn nx typecheck web).

tasks.title: Build MarketingHero — image-band hero for Online/Offline Courses

spec.purpose: Full-bleed image-band hero used by both /online-courses (1:1547) and /offline-courses (1:4756). One component, two prop sets.

layout.container: Full-bleed section. Background image rendered via next/image fill with object-cover. Foreground content centered horizontally, padded vertically (py-20 md:py-28 lg:py-32).
layout.content_column: max-w-3xl mx-auto text-center on the foreground.
layout.stack: eyebrow (text-mini uppercase, text-text-brand) -> headline (text-h2, text-text-primary or text-text-inverse depending on bg contrast) -> subtitle (text-subtext-1 or text-body-md, text-text-secondary).

spec.responsive: Mobile: py-16, headline scales via existing text-h2 token (already responsive). md: py-20. lg: py-28. Background image uses next/image sizes='100vw'.
non_goals: No overlay tint (this is the bright image-band variant, NOT the dark-overlay ListingHero).
No CTA button (Figma shows no button in this hero).
Do not hardcode colors — only tokens.

acceptance: Component is a named export `MarketingHero` from apps/web/src/components/sections/marketing-hero.tsx.
Renders eyebrow, headline, subtitle in that vertical order with the tokens above; uses no inline hex.
Background image is full-bleed via next/image fill, with object-cover and sizes='100vw'.
At md breakpoint the content remains centered; at sm the stack still reads top-to-bottom without horizontal overflow.
TypeScript compiles. Renders inside a page route without runtime errors.

tasks.title: Build ListingHero — dark-overlay hero for /tips and /online-advanced-certifications

spec.purpose: Single dark-overlay image hero shared by the two lean listing pages. Supports both the editorial variant (eyebrow + accent-span headline) and the breadcrumb variant (breadcrumb + result-count) via optional props.

props_shape.headline: string
props_shape.headlineAccent?: string (rendered as a trailing <span> in text-brand-shade)
props_shape.subtitle: string

layout.container: Full-bleed section, min-h-[420px] md:min-h-[460px]. Background image via next/image fill object-cover. Absolute dark overlay (bg-black/80 by default) above image. Content stacked above overlay, centered, max-w-4xl mx-auto.
layout.stack: (eyebrow || breadcrumb) -> headline with optional accent span -> subtitle -> optional resultCount.
layout.headline_styling: text-h1 text-text-inverse. headlineAccent renders inline as a <span className='text-brand-shade'> after a space.

spec.responsive: min-h-[360px] sm, [420px] md, [460px] lg. Headline relies on responsive text-h1 token. Subtitle wraps with max-w-2xl.
non_goals: Do NOT introduce a new --color-brand-mint token; use text-brand-shade for the accent unless design QA flags otherwise (per scout note).
Do not hardcode rgba — use bg-black/80 Tailwind utility for the overlay.

acceptance: Component is a named export `ListingHero` from apps/web/src/components/sections/listing-hero.tsx.
When eyebrow is provided, renders an uppercased eyebrow above headline; when breadcrumb is provided, renders breadcrumb instead (both optional).
When headlineAccent is provided, the accent span renders inline after the base headline in text-brand-shade.
Dark overlay sits between bg image and content; overlay opacity is controlled by bg-black/80 (default) and content remains readable (text-text-inverse).
When resultCount is provided, renders below the subtitle in text-mini uppercase.
TypeScript compiles; renders inside a page without runtime errors; mobile breakpoint keeps content stacked without overflow.

tasks.title: Build FilterChipBar — 3-pill tab indicator for /online-courses

spec.purpose: Three rounded-full pill buttons acting as a tab/filter selector. Used standalone above the course grid on /online-courses. Controlled OR uncontrolled.
spec.behavior: If activeIndex is provided, render controlled. Else maintain internal useState seeded by defaultIndex. Clicking a pill calls onChange with the new index and updates internal state when uncontrolled.

layout.container: Inline-flex row, gap-2, rounded-full bg-surface-2 p-1.
layout.pill_active: bg-brand-primary text-text-inverse rounded-full px-4 py-1.5 text-mini uppercase tracking-wide transition-colors.
layout.pill_inactive: bg-transparent text-text-secondary rounded-full px-4 py-1.5 text-mini uppercase tracking-wide hover:text-text-primary.

spec.accessibility: Render as <div role='tablist'> with each pill as <button role='tab' aria-selected={isActive}>. Keyboard: tabbing moves focus; Enter/Space activate.
non_goals: Do NOT wire this to a router or query param — pure presentational; consumer page can wire state later.
Do not implement underline animation; the spec is pill-based per design.

acceptance: Component is a named export `FilterChipBar` from apps/web/src/components/ui/filter-chip-bar.tsx.
Renders one button per tab in the order provided.
Active pill has bg-brand-primary and text-text-inverse; inactive pills are transparent with text-text-secondary. No inline hex.
Works in both controlled (activeIndex + onChange) and uncontrolled (defaultIndex) modes — verified by usage on demo page.
Each pill has role='tab' and aria-selected reflecting active state.
TypeScript compiles; mobile renders without overflow (wrap or scroll-x-auto acceptable).

acceptance: Page route resolves at /online-courses with no runtime errors (verify by running yarn nx dev web and visiting the URL, or via build).

ProgramCards inside PopularCoursesSection render the floating Online mode badge (T1 extension).

demo_pages.note: The four production routes themselves serve as the visual-qa surfaces; no separate /demo routes are required per task. visual-qa will screenshot /online-courses, /offline-courses, /tips-to-become-a-successful-yoga-teacher, /online-advanced-certifications.
optional_isolation_routes: apps/web/src/app/demo/listing-hero/page.tsx — mount ListingHero in both eyebrow and breadcrumb variants for isolated QA
apps/web/src/app/demo/filter-chip-bar/page.tsx — mount FilterChipBar in controlled + uncontrolled modes

global_acceptance_criteria: All components and pages use only existing tokens from apps/web/src/app/globals.css (text-h1..h5, text-mini, text-subtext-*, text-body-*, text-text-*, bg-*, border-border-*, shadow-card, font-*). NO inline hex, NO clamp().
All files compile under TypeScript (yarn nx typecheck web).
All four page routes render without runtime errors and have valid Next.js Metadata exports.
Mobile breakpoint (sm) stacks all sections vertically with no horizontal scroll on a 375px viewport.
Tablet breakpoint (md) reflows grids 3->2 col where applicable.
Desktop (lg/xl/2xl) matches the 1920px Figma intent via max-w-7xl or max-w-[1472px] container.
warnings_carried_forward: FilterChipBar labels (All / Online / Offline) are inferred from Figma — confirm with design during visual-qa.
Footer is not in Figma frame 1:2343 — we mount SiteFooter anyway per page-shell convention.
Mint accent on ListingHero uses existing text-brand-shade (#8ee0ce); design may flag the brighter #3fffd5 — defer adding a new token until QA flags it.
ArticleCard is NOT a new file — it is ProgramCard with variant='article'. Do not create apps/web/src/components/ui/article-card.tsx.
